calls.quote: I bought two brand new stocks that I think can double before the end of this year. The first stock is Root, Ticker ROOT, which is an insurance company which rivals one of my favorite stocks actually, Lemonade. They're currently only trading at 1.03x their sales which just screams undervalued. On top of that, they're growing their revenues by 32.4% year over year and they're actually a profitable company right now with zero debt. Honestly, I'm bullish on both Lemonade and Root because there's much needed disruption right now in the insurance space.
calls.summary: The creator bought Root, an insurance company, citing undervaluation at 1.03x sales, 32.4% revenue growth, profitability, and zero debt, as a high-risk high-reward play.

calls.quote: The next stock I bought was Fubo, which is a streaming service. Today, their shareholders just approved the plan to merge with Disney. Now that was expected. That was the easy part. The real approval process lies with the Department of Justice and this can happen three to six months from now. If Fubo gets approved to merge with Disney, this stock is gonna go flying through the roof.
calls.summary: The creator bought Fubo, a streaming service, expecting a price surge if the Disney merger is approved by the Department of Justice.
